content: Real-world ILS capture/autopilot logic: localizer should be intercepted within roughly 30° of the published inbound course to avoid false/erroneous captures; the system 'arms' on LOC/APPR mode and only transitions to active capture once the signal is stably within threshold, executing a single coordinated turn onto the localizer without overshooting the centerline null. Glideslope should be captured from below (flying level or shallow-descent until glideslope intercept), not from above (avoids false glideslope lobes and excessive descent rates at capture). Deviation is expressed as an angular signal from the transmitter and converted to a linear/rectilinear deviation using estimated distance to the transmitter — near the antenna, small angular deviations represent large lateral/vertical errors, and far away, the reverse — a common simulation bug is treating angular deviation as if it were linear at all ranges.
